headline: How The ADT (ADT) Narrative Is Shifting As Analysts Reframe Risk And Reward
summary: ADT is back in focus as analysts tighten their price targets into a narrower US$7 to US$9 range, while one fair value anchor remains unchanged at US$8.10 per share. These moves reflect a reassessment of how much risk to accept as the company works through distribution changes, higher investment, and a mix of bullish and bearish views on its path ahead. Read on to see how this evolving narrative might shape the way you track ADT from here. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full...

headline: Bond Traders Set Up for Jobs Data With Iran War Still Main Focus
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Bond traders torn between growth and inflation concerns are setting up for a key read on the US jobs market, even as the continuing hostilities in the Middle East remain the center of attention. The data is expected to show a moderate rebound in hiring last month and an unchanged unemployment rate. It’s a steady picture that would likely do little to alter expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate policy, with investors more focused lately on events in Iran and the surge in o

headline: These 10 stocks are poised for earnings surprises in April: Morgan Stanley
summary: Investing.com-- Morgan Stanley is highlighting a fresh set of U.S. and European stocks with the strongest earnings surprise potential ahead of the upcoming reporting season, using a quantitative framework that blends analyst ratings with forward-looking signals. The bank’s approach combines metrics from its Earnings Forecast Landscape, Earnings Quality, and broader forecast dynamics to construct what it calls an Earnings Surprise Composite. headline: Citigroup (C) Valuation In Focus As Long Term Returns Contrast With Recent Share Performance
summary: Citigroup stock in focus Citigroup (C) is back on investors’ radar as its recent share performance, including a modest move over the past week and mixed returns over the past 3 months and year to date, prompts a closer look at fundamentals. See our latest analysis for Citigroup. Citigroup’s recent 7-day share price return of 2.53% and 30-day share price return of 4.05% sit against a softer 90-day share price return of 2.91%.
